meta.title: Lev Graph-OS — The Real Thing
meta.description: How a 4-day false-positive loop produced a graph kernel that nobody could use — and the design interview that fixed it.

props.title: We Built the Wrong Thing for 4 Days. Then We Stopped.
props.subtitle: A design interview that turned 14 graph adapters, 36 ratchet tests, and 5,393 garbage wiki pages into a real product definition.

props.content: We had a kernel that compiled. We had pentagon tests passing across 11 production-relevant adapters. We had a ratchet handler with atomic writes, a ViewEngine, an event-driven canon trigger, and a scheduled flowmind ready to fire. And no human or agent could actually use any of it. This is what happened when we stopped shipping plumbing and asked the question we should have asked on day one: what is this thing FOR?

props.label: THE EPIPHANY
props.title: Graph isn't the source of truth. The world model is.

props.content:
For four days we ran adversarial validation rounds against the graph kernel. Each round 'proved' the kernel. Each round was the kernel grading itself. False-positive loop.

Then the operator asked: *what's the actual product? what does an agent or human invoke? where does this pay rent?*

The honest answer was: nothing, nobody, and nowhere. The kernel was correct against itself. It had no consumer.

The interview that followed forced a re-grounding. The graph isn't truth. The graph is hygiene + virtual filesystem. **Truth lives in the world model** (and in code, for SDLC). The graph is what makes that truth queryable, projectable, and gate-able. Like Windows maintains indexes and macOS has special system folders, lev's `.lev/` is a virtualized filesystem with explicit sync semantics. Graph-OS is the right name.
We built a graph that compiled. Now we're building a graph that's used.

props.title: Current
props.content: **14 adapters** scaffolded, **11 READY**, but no operator surface. `lev graph` CLI returns 'Unknown command'. 5,393 markdown 'wiki' pages — 3,406 are FK-stub trash. ViewEngine works programmatically, no MCP exposure. Ratchet writes to canon — but only via test harness. SDLC has 51 lifecycle violations surfaced — operator can't see them. Zero end-to-end loops. Plumbing without product.

props.title: Ideal
props.content: **One kernel substrate**, N bucket schemas, 11 rtype overlays. Multi-source agentic-CMS funnel: bd / agent / human / autowiki / intake / cass / code → compositor → kernel. Markdown files are projections, regenerable. Per-bucket gate suites: pentagon for SDLC (adversarial CLI/MCP/SDK + spawn-agent-per-flow-branch), lint/orphan/health for wiki. Hot-path sync policies (instant / lazy / reactive). DNA cascade as canon (already proven 8 nodes / 26 min / $7.37). World model owns truth. Graph owns hygiene.

There are **8 primitives** in the kernel: entity, claim, evidence, proposal, gate, view, tick, intent. They never change. ADR-002 locked **4 node types** for the runtime: agent, function, gate, subgraph. Cannot extend.

On top of that we have **11 rtype overlays** — these are the cross-cutting facets that compose. lifecycle, execution, context, memory, skill, discourse, code, research, wiki, simulation, blackboard. The same entity can be tagged with multiple rtypes. A design document is `discourse + wiki`. A spec is `lifecycle + execution`. A flowmind YAML is `execution`. A wiki page is `wiki + discourse + memory`.

Over THAT we have **buckets** — typed content categories with their own schema and AGENT_SPEC. Buckets already exist underused at `.lev/pm/<bucket>/_template/schema.json`: proposal, design, spec, decision, plan, handoff, report. We're adding wiki-page, article, video, workstream, receipt.

**Modifications are first-class.** A `proposal` is the post-interview artifact that names: which N entities to patch, and which domain plugins make it real (the execution topology). A proposal that updates a website with a new header video would touch `plugins/video` AND `plugins/sdlc` simultaneously — that's why overlays matter.

DNA cascade is the propagation pattern: edit `dna/core/<x>` → compositor 4-phase fires → rtype overlays react → plugin owners activate → pentagon gates green or tmux teleport on red. Already shipped end-to-end.

props.title: The 11 rtype overlays · who owns them · what they compose

props.label: THE STACK
props.title: Three layers + a world model on the side

props.content:
graph TD
    subgraph Sources[Authoring sources · multi-source funnel]
      BD[bd CLI] --> COMP
      AGENT[Agent SDK/MCP] --> COMP
      HUMAN[Human CLI] --> COMP
      AW[autowiki intake] --> COMP
      CODE[code-graph extract] --> COMP
      CASS[cass session capture] --> COMP
    end
    COMP[Compositor 4-phase · extract / classify / validate / apply] --> KERNEL
    KERNEL[Kernel · 8 primitives · 11 rtypes · ratchet-admission]
    KERNEL --> WM[World Model · V-JEPA + r2dreamer · on_step admission]
    WM -.cedes truth back.-> KERNEL
    KERNEL --> PROJ
    subgraph Projections[Read-only views]
      MD[Markdown files · .lev/wiki/ · .lev/pm/]
      HTML[lev-now HTML publishes]
      FLOW[FlowMind execution graphs]
      CTX[Prompt context packs]
      DASH[Dashboards / GenUI]
      PUB[Public sites · graph-os.ai]
    end
    PROJ[Projection layer] --> MD
    PROJ --> HTML
    PROJ --> FLOW
    PROJ --> CTX
    PROJ --> DASH
    PROJ --> PUB
props.caption: Lev Graph-OS architecture (2026-05-04). Kernel is one substrate. World model owns predictive truth. Files are projections.

props.content: The kernel is the source. The world model has the actual truth (predicted state). Projections are the read-only file tree you see in your editor. Edits go through the SDK. The file watcher exists for legacy markdown not as the canonical write path.

props.title: 🧱 plugin-scaffolder
props.content: `lev plugin new <name>` generates a DNA-validated pentagon scaffold via compositor. README + handler stubs + tests + flowmind. Surfaces: CLI + SDK + MCP.

props.title: 🧠 memory-recall
props.content: Cross-session graph traversal answers "what did we decide about X?" with provenance. Multi-hop, cite-or-refuse policy. Surfaces: CLI + MCP + SDK.

props.title: 📥 workshop-promote
props.content: `lev intake <repo>` → license/dep audit → pentagon promotion proposal. The path from external clone to first-class plugin. Surfaces: CLI + MCP + SDK.

props.title: 👥 multi-agent-design-review
props.content: Parallel architect / critic / security / perf reviewers compose verdicts. Ratchet admits or rejects. Surfaces: CLI + MCP + SDK.

props.title: 📡 observe-runtime
props.content: `lev observe` opens a live dashboard graphing flow execution, gate fires, plugin event traffic. Tmux teleport on failure. Surfaces: CLI + MCP + SDK + Web.

props.title: 🎯 skill-loop
props.content: `lev skill new` → write → run → telemetry → evaluator suggests SKILL.md improvements. Skills evolve. Surfaces: CLI + SDK + MCP.

props.title: 📰 auto-publish-capture
props.content: Session close → graphify → project → publish to lev-now with entity links and receipts. Surfaces: CLI + MCP + SDK + Web.

props.title: 🌊 dna-cascade-explorer
props.content: Stage a DNA edit, GenUI shows cascade preview before commit (which entities transition, which gates fire, which plugins activate). Surfaces: CLI + MCP + SDK + GenUI.

props.title: 🚦 router-bench
props.content: Test bench for the conversational router. See which skill/flow it routes to, why, and what the alternatives were. Surfaces: CLI + MCP + SDK.

props.title: 🌳 worktree-continuity
props.content: Across multiple parallel branches, `lev work` shows live workstream state, what each agent is touching, conflicts predicted. Surfaces: CLI + MCP + SDK + GenUI.

props.label: 10 IRL USE CASES
props.title: What lev does for the rest of the world
props.subtitle: Scaffolded at community/examples/irl/ — enterprise → SMB → grade school

props.title: 🏀 Grade-school basketball league
props.content: 47 entities. 6 role projections (parent / coach / kid-safe player / admin / sponsor / referee). RBAC over one graph. Coach app has AI assistant via MCP. SMS/email via Twilio. Same graph, different surfaces.

props.title: 📊 Prediction market API
props.content: 10 named flowminds: data-ingest, market-creation, order-routing, oracle-resolution, payout, anti-fraud, settlement, audit, public-projection, alerting. Cron + webhooks + multi-language poly SDKs. Pure flowmind orchestration.

props.title: 🍽️ Restaurant chain inventory
props.content: Multi-location, supplier APIs, par levels, waste tracking, predictive reordering. Manager app, supplier portal, finance dashboard. EDI to multiple suppliers.

props.title: 🏠 Real estate agent CRM
props.content: Leads → showings → offers → close. MLS sync, drip campaigns, comps, e-sign. Agent app, broker dashboard, client portal, lender portal.

props.title: 💉 Med spa appointment + treatment
props.content: HIPAA. Bookings, treatment plans, before/after photos with privacy gradient, supply tracking, lot/recall. PHI access audit log differentiates each role.

props.title: 🏭 Software dark factory
props.content: Auto PR triage, design→spec→code→test→deploy with human-in-loop only at decision points. Full SDLC graph + pentagon. Lev 0.1.0 makes this trivial.

props.title: 🎙️ Podcast producer studio
props.content: Guest pipeline → recording → transcript → publish → social distribution → analytics. Producer app, guest portal, public site.

props.title: 🎓 University TA management
props.content: Courses, sections, TAs, office hours, grading workflow, regrade, plagiarism. Faculty dashboard, TA app, student app.

props.title: 💍 Wedding planner
props.content: Vendors, timeline, budget, guest list, RSVPs, day-of run sheet. Planner dashboard, couple app, vendor portal, guest invite site.

props.title: 🚧 Construction daily log
props.content: Crews, equipment, materials, weather, safety incidents, photos, client weekly digest. Foreman app, office portal, client read-only site.

props.label: ONE GRAPH · MANY EYES
props.title: Basketball League — one graph, six role projections

props.content: Same 47-entity graph. Six different role projections. The parent sees their kid's schedule and injury notes. The coach sees the practice plan + player stats + scouting reports. The player sees a kid-safe view (no liability waivers, no payment data). The admin sees the league-wide finances and incident reports. The sponsor sees their tier benefits and brand placement. The referee sees only assigned games + scorekeeping. **One write surface, six reads. RBAC enforced at the projection layer, audit log records every cross-role view.** This is the graph-os promise made concrete.

props.title: Six roles · what each one sees

rows.sees: Their child's schedule, injury reports, payment status
rows.blocked: Other families' data, sponsor finances, league internal

rows.sees: Roster, lineup, practice plans, player stats, scouting
rows.blocked: Liability waivers, payment data outside team
rows.surface: Coach app (AI assistant via MCP) + web

rows.sees: Tier benefits, brand placement, payment history
rows.blocked: Player medical, parent contact details, league internal
Player stats outside their game, league finances

items.title: Adapter pentagon spike designed
items.description: 4-port contract (StoragePort / GraphPort / Projector / RetrievalStrategy). 6-adapter scaffolds. Pentagon harness shape locked.

items.title: AAVF Round 1-3 — kernel canon ratified
items.description: 8 primitives + hybrid IR + 5-state TruthState + locality-default-on. Composition A2×B3×C3×D2×E1 ratified.

items.title: AAVF perf round + lev-now publish
items.description: Real LLM bench (~$0.005/battery), sqlite 60x P99 fix, full battery published to here.now.

items.title: Round 5 stress + ratchet path + close-loop
items.description: D1/D2 stress sub-test, ratchet-admission gate, ViewEngine, event-watcher, scheduler entry. 36 ratchet tests, atomic-write bug fixed.

items.title: Pentagon battery against 14 adapters
items.description: 11/14 READY 1.00 (bd, code, file, falkordb, neo4j, memory, lineage, cognee, graphiti, lightrag, surrealdb). 2 SKIPPED-cleanly (trustgraph, nanograph). 1 delegate (discourse).

items.title: Operator stops the bus: 'what is this thing FOR?'
items.description: False-positive loop named. Plumbing without product. Stop building. Start interviewing.

items.title: Design interview · this capture
items.description: 11 decisions ratified. 20 use cases scaffolded. Canonical design at docs/design/. 3 final questions remain before propose.

items.title: Answer the 3 final questions
items.description: Hot-path sync policy. Workstream authority precedence. Wiki + article + video bucket schema fusion.

items.title: Propose: wiki proof slice + workstream-as-entity migration
items.description: First proposal artifact: graph patch ops + execution topology naming domain plugins. Dogfood the new agentic-CMS pattern.

items.title: Wiki proof slice ships
items.description: plugins/wiki (renamed from autowiki). .lev/wiki/. Lint/orphan/health gates. The first wiki node is THIS design.

items.title: Stale-doc redirect campaign
items.description: Purge 5,393 stub-polluted wiki pages. Rename autowiki → wiki. Mark 'graph-cms IS substrate' captures superseded by ADR-0001.

items.title: lev graph CLI registration unblocks
items.description: The P1 from 2026-05-01 graph-crud-audit. Fractal-scanner registration fix. 15 handlers become reachable from `lev graph`.

items.title: Workstream-as-entity migration
items.description: Today's static .lev/pm/workstreams/<id>/state/workstream.yaml becomes a graph-tracked entity. Entity-load order recorded live during sessions.

items.title: SDLC proof slice (proposal/design/spec/plan/decision)
items.description: Reuse existing plugins/sdlc + sdlc-pentagon harness. Lifecycle gates against graph state, NOT files. 51 violations from W2-E become actionable.

items.title: First IRL use case scaffolds become real demos
items.description: Basketball league: prove the same-graph-different-roles UX. Prediction market: prove the 10-flowmind chain.

items.title: Lev 0.1.0 — software dark factory becomes trivial
items.description: All buckets schemed. All cascades pentagonized. World model wired in via on_step. Public sites publish from graph-os.ai.

props.title: Eight artifacts marked stale or to-be-redirected

rows.action: Purge per W3-A pivot. Regenerate via wiki proof slice.
Rename to plugins/wiki. Update flow files, schedule files, all references.
Move to .lev/pm/specs/_archive/2026-02-poly-runtime/. Extract M1-M10 misses to backlog.
Promote to spec OR mark superseded.
Migrate (apply-overlay handler shipped, dna/thesis.yaml says it's time).
Mark superseded by ADR-0001 tribunal unanimous B (2026-04-15).
Mark stale. D3 in 2026-04-29 canon-decisions ratified `lev work {assign|start|release}`.

props.content: Lev Graph-OS is not a product yet. It's a kernel + a 14-adapter pentagon + a real product definition that we just finished writing. The first 30 days produce a real wiki, a real SDLC loop, and the first basketball league running on graph-os. The boulder rolls.

props.title: 🛠️ For builders
props.content: Read `docs/design/design-graph-stack-product-definition.md`. Pick a use case from `community/examples/` and turn it into a real demo. Open a PR.

props.title: 🔬 For researchers
props.content: The world-model layer is unbuilt. V-JEPA + r2dreamer is scaffolded. The on_step admission seam is ready. Pull the design at `.lev/pm/designs/design-world-model-stack.md`.

props.title: 🏀 For operators
props.content: The basketball league use case is the simplest end-to-end path. 47 entities. 6 role projections. RBAC via plugins/abac. Build it. Become the first lev customer that isn't lev.

props.label: APPENDIX
props.title: Decisions D1–D11 ratified in this interview
D1 · One kernel substrate
props.content: Not 5 substrates. Not file-tree-as-source. ONE kernel + N bucket schemas + 11 rtype overlays.

props.title: D2 · 4 ADR-002 node types are locked
props.content: agent / function / gate / subgraph. Cannot extend. DNA cascade is propagation pattern, not node type.

props.title: D3 · Beads is reference pattern only
props.content: Custom-content-types come from `.lev/<bucket>/_template/schema.json`. Build our own SDK. Beads stays as a study target.

props.title: D4 · Pentagon is SDLC-specific
props.content: Adversarial test of execution surfaces (CLI / MCP / SDK + spawn-agent-per-flow-branch). Not for wiki.

props.title: D5 · Wiki gates differ
props.content: Lint / orphan / health (autowiki materialization + discourse style). Different gate suite per bucket type.

props.title: D6 · Proposal in the new world
props.content: Post-interview artifact. Two parts: graph patch ops on N nodes + execution topology naming domain plugins.

props.title: D7 · Overlays compose multi-rtype proposals
props.content: A website update with new header video touches plugins/video AND plugins/sdlc simultaneously. That's why overlays matter.

props.title: D8 · Graph is hygiene, world model is truth
props.content: SDLC truth = code + graph (graph for hygiene). Other domains have their own truth substrates the graph references but does not own.

props.title: D9 · Graph-OS = OS file system semantics
props.content: .lev/ is a virtual filesystem with explicit sync policies. Code targets are user-declared hot paths (instant / lazy / reactive).

props.title: D10 · First slice = wiki + workstream lifecycle
props.content: Plan/act/verify loop preserved. Get out of markdown hell on the bucket types we already maintain by hand.

props.title: D11 · Autowiki + openbrain fusion
props.content: Karpathy materialization + Nat B Jones structured data. Best of both: originals immutable, LLM extracts structure not prose, projections regenerable. https://youtu.be/dxq7WtWxi44
